body:
## Related Issues

After reviewing existing issues and PRs, this use case intersects with several ongoing discussions:

- **#5533** - [Feature] Conditionally disable modules based on environment variables
- **#6604** - [Directory]: update `truncate_to_repo` to work with git worktrees  
- **#4439** (PR) - feat: allow loading configuration values from the environment (open since 2022)
- **#840** - Activating modules dynamically

The core limitation is that `when` clauses only work on custom modules, not built-ins like `directory`. 

If #4439 were merged, a workaround would be possible via fish/bash wrapper that sets `STARSHIP_CONFIG__DIRECTORY__DISABLED=true` when detecting worktree + matching dir/branch. But native support would be cleaner.

This might also be addressed by extending `detect_env_vars` (which exists for some modules) to the `directory` module, combined with shell-side worktree detection.
